{{Infobox group
| name = Monarchs
| introduced = Skysworn
}}

''See also: [[Monarchs Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|Monarchs are the most powerful individuals in their world, serving as legendary figures that many mortals view as myths. They function as the supreme rulers of vast territories and powerful clans, such as the [[Akura]] family or the [[House Shen]]. While they possess immense strength—capable of fighting [[Dreadgods]] to a stalemate and shattering fortresses with mere words—they exist in a delicate equilibrium. They must balance their power to avoid provoking the [[Dreadgods]] into collective action, often maintaining a stable population of mortals to sustain their rule. Monarchs possess extraordinary abilities, including the capacity to tear open spatial rifts, see vast distances, and hear their names being spoken from across the globe. Their presence is so significant that they can project their will or form through sand, and even their [[Remnants]] are considered priceless treasures. They often oversee grand traditions, such as tournaments for their disciples to identify future leaders. Despite their near-godlike status, they face significant burdens, including the responsibility of protecting civilization and managing the threat of [[hunger aura]]. Reaching this stage typically requires ascending from the ranks of [[Sages]] or [[Heralds]], a process that is both highly sought after and fraught with difficulty.}}

== Skysworn ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Monarchs]] are beings whose whims lives depend on.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c6">''Skysworn'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Monarchs]] are the most powerful individuals in the world.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c7">''Skysworn'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Monarchs]] can temporarily destroy a [[Dreadgod]]'s body.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c18">''Skysworn'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Monarchs]] are beings that battle [[Dreadgods]].<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c18" />}}

== Ghostwater ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|[[Harmony]] can see as [[Monarchs]] do.<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c18">''Ghostwater'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|The upcoming competition will be a battle between [[Monarchs]].<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c19">''Ghostwater'', chapter 19</ref>}}

== Underlord ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|[[Monarchs]] are a level of power or entity mentioned by [[Eithan]].<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c5">''Underlord'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|[[Eithan]] believes [[Dross]] will eventually tower over [[Monarchs]].<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c8">''Underlord'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|All current [[Monarchs]] will be in attendance at the upcoming tournament.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c13">''Underlord'', chapter 13</ref>}}

== Uncrowned ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Twelve]] [[Monarchs]] attacked the [[Wandering Titan]] during the [[Dread War]].<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c15">''Uncrowned'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Monarchs]] in [[Cradle]] have refused recruitment by the [[Abidan]].<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c21">''Uncrowned'', chapter 21</ref>}}

== Wintersteel ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The Monarchs are spirits who once scanned [[Yerin]] and have historically been identified by their wearing of blue badges. During recent events, many members of this group made peculiar remarks regarding the [[Ninecloud team]]. While they can become upset if their view is interrupted, their collective attention serves as a prize for a victor and possesses the power to restore a damaged individual. Furthermore, they are capable of reversing injuries by rewriting the fundamental principles of [[madra]].}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|Many [[Monarchs]] have made strange comments about the [[Ninecloud team]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c3">''Wintersteel'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The collective attention of the [[Monarchs]] can restore a damaged individual.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c4">''Wintersteel'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The attention of the [[Monarchs]] is the prize for a victor.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Monarchs]] can be upset by interruptions to their view.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c6">''Wintersteel'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Monarchs]] can reverse injuries through a process involving rewriting the fundamental principles of [[madra]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c20">''Wintersteel'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Monarchs]] are spirits that scanned [[Yerin]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c26">''Wintersteel'', chapter 26</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Monarchs]] historically wore badges of blue.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c28">''Wintersteel'', chapter 28</ref>}}

== Bloodline ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Yerin]] is sick of [[Monarchs]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c14">''Bloodline'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Dreadgod]] sees eight [[Monarchs]] hovering over [[Mount Samara]] in a vision.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c20">''Bloodline'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Among the [[Monarchs]], only one claims to have influence over the [[Dreadgods]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c21">''Bloodline'', chapter 21</ref>}}

== Reaper ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The secrets of the Monarchs were offered to [[Lindon]] by [[Elder Whisper]], leading him to wonder why these powerful beings had not intervened regarding the anchor. While their attacks or those of the [[Dreadgods]] have physically scarred the territory surrounding [[Refuge]], [[Eithan]] asserts that his own schemes are enough to make even Monarchs tremble. These entities benefit from the existing system and have threatened to punish the team should they enter the [[labyrinth]], though they are known to clash over various pieces of advice. As controllers of the [[Dreadgods]]' records, it is established that the [[Dreadgods]] will only perish once all Monarchs have died. Ultimately, the Monarchs are involved in the scenes remade within the [[World of Night]], and [[Lindon]] intends to drag them along with him as he advances.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Elder Whisper]] offered [[Lindon]] the secrets of [[Monarchs]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c2">''Reaper'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Lindon]] questions why the [[Monarchs]] have not taken action regarding the anchor.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Attacks from [[Dreadgods]] or [[Monarchs]] have carved ravines into the territory around [[Refuge]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c4">''Reaper'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Eithan]] claims his machinations make [[Monarchs]] tremble.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c8">''Reaper'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Monarchs]] will punish the team if they enter the [[labyrinth]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c9">''Reaper'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Monarchs]] benefit from the current system.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c10">''Reaper'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Monarchs]] can clash over advice.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c21">''Reaper'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Monarchs]] control the records of the [[Dreadgods]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c22">''Reaper'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Dreadgods]] will die only when there are no more [[Monarchs]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Monarchs]] are part of the scenes remade in the [[World of Night]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c26">''Reaper'', chapter 26</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Lindon]] intends to drag the [[Monarchs]] with him when he advances.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c27">''Reaper'', chapter 27</ref>}}

== Dreadgod ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The Monarchs are a group of powerful beings whose very existence upsets the natural balance. While they represent a looming threat to [[Lindon]], they are also viewed as potential sponsors who may arrive at any time. Their presence sustains the [[Dreadgods]], leading to conspiracy theories that the two forces are working together; indeed, the [[Abidan]] consider these beings to be parasites. The Monarchs are connected to a [[hunger aura]] that occurs when they remain in one place for too long, though the [[Heralds]] note they are not currently being observed. During conflicts involving the [[Silent King]], other Monarchs were seen wrestling against his workings, even as the [[Blood Sage]] claimed to have bestowed power capable of rivaling them.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Monarchs]] are expected to come for [[Lindon]] soon.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c2">''Dreadgod'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Monarchs]] represent a looming threat to [[Lindon]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Heralds]] mention they are not being observed by the [[Monarchs]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c3">''Dreadgod'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The presence of the [[Monarchs]] sustains the [[Dreadgods]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c4">''Dreadgod'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Lindon]] is waiting for trouble from [[Monarchs]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c5">''Dreadgod'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Monarchs]] are potential sponsors.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c5" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Monarchs]] are connected to [[hunger aura]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c11">''Dreadgod'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Monarchs]] are a group of powerful beings.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c12">''Dreadgod'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The existence of [[Monarchs]] upsets a sort of balance.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c12" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Blood Sage]] claimed to have bestowed power that could rival [[Monarchs]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c15">''Dreadgod'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|Other [[Monarchs]] were wrestling against the workings of the [[Silent King]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c19">''Dreadgod'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Monarchs]] are letting the [[Dreadgods]] stick around.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c19" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Abidan]] consider [[Monarchs]] to be parasites.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c21">''Dreadgod'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Monarchs]] staying too long causes [[hunger aura]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|There is a conspiracy theory that [[Monarchs]] and the [[Dreadgods]] are working together.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c23">''Dreadgod'', chapter 23</ref>}}

== Waybound ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|As the kings and queens of the world, these powerful entities are capable of fighting [[Dreadgods]] and often utilize time-warped pocket dimensions or cycle for years at a time. While they financed certain operations, their presence necessitates the use of soul oaths to prevent their rise. These beings require immense willpower to combat, as even their casual exchanges consist of high-level techniques, and they were once struck by the breath of a black dragon. During a mythic war, [[Lindon]] challenged them in an effort to remove them from the world, as they were determined to deny him any time to prepare.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Monarchs]] financed the operation.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c2">''Waybound'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The characters expect to fight among [[Monarchs]] soon.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Monarchs]] are powerful entities that require willpower to fight.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c3">''Waybound'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Yerin]] has faced down the [[Monarchs]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Monarchs]] use time-warped pocket dimensions.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c7">''Waybound'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Monarchs]] are known to cycle for years at a time.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Sword Sage]] mentions tickling [[Monarchs]] in their secrets.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c8">''Waybound'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Monarchs]] were hit by [[black dragon's breath]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c9">''Waybound'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Monarchs]] can use techniques that are casual exchanges on their level.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c10">''Waybound'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Monarchs]] are determined to deny [[Lindon]] time to prepare.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c11">''Waybound'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Lindon]] challenged the [[Monarchs]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Lindon]] is fighting [[Monarchs]] in a mythic war.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c12">''Waybound'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Lindon]] needs time once the [[Monarchs]] are gone.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c23">''Waybound'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Monarchs]] must be removed from the world.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c23" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Monarchs]] are the kings and queens of the world capable of fighting [[Dreadgods]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c27">''Waybound'', chapter 27</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The purpose of the soul oath is to prevent the rise of [[Monarchs]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c33">''Waybound'', chapter 33</ref>}}

== Threshold: Stories from Cradle ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Monarchs]] award prizes in the tournament.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c1">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|The [[Monarchs]] are watching the events.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c1" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|Combat between [[Monarchs]] can cause spatial cracks.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c10">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Lindon]] tells the remaining [[Monarchs]] they must ascend or [[Eithan]] will kill them.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c14">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 14</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|“I offered you the secrets of Monarchs, young Lindon.”<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c2" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|“For one thing, if you know about this, why haven’t the Monarchs taken action?”<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c2" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|“Even if we get what we’re after and make it out, the Monarchs will punish us.”<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c9" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|“You’re waiting for some trouble from Monarchs.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c5" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|"Oh, so it’s about the Monarchs letting the Dreadgods stick around."<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c19" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Cradle nav}}

[[Category:Groups]]
[[Category:Skysworn]]
[[Category:Ghostwater]]
[[Category:Underlord]]
[[Category:Uncrowned]]
[[Category:Wintersteel]]
[[Category:Bloodline]]
[[Category:Reaper]]
[[Category:Dreadgod]]
[[Category:Waybound]]
[[Category:Threshold: Stories from Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle groups]]
